hierarchy.title: <image>
hierarchy.text:
<image>
RBI/FMRD/2023-24/109 FMRD.DIRD.09/14.02.001/2023-24
January 03, 2024 To
All Eligible Market Participants
Madam/Sir

Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Paper and Non- Convertible Debentures of original or initial maturity upto one year) Directions, 2024
Please refer to paragraph 6 of the Statement on Developmental and Regulatory
Policies, announced as a part of the second Bi-monthly Monetary Policy Statement for
2019-20 dated June 06, 2019 regarding Comprehensive Review of Money Market
Directions. Accordingly, the draft Directions on Call, Notice and Term Money,
Certificate of Deposit and the Commercial Paper and Non-Convertible Debentures of
original or initial maturity upto one year markets were released for market feedback on
December 04, 2020. The Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Call, Notice and
Term Money Markets) Directions, 2021 and the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of
India (Certificate of Deposit) Directions, 2021 were issued on April 01, 2021 and June
04, 2021 respectively.

hierarchy.title: 2. The Directions on Commercial Paper and Non-Convertible Debentures of original
hierarchy.text:
2. The Directions on Commercial Paper and Non-Convertible Debentures of original
or initial maturity upto one year have been reviewed based on market feedback and
the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Paper and Non-Convertible
Debentures of original or initial maturity upto one year) Directions, 2024 are being
issued herewith.

hierarchy.title: 3. These Directions have been issued in exercise of the powers conferred under
hierarchy.text:
3. These Directions have been issued in exercise of the powers conferred under
section 45J, 45K, 45L and 45W of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 read with
section 45U of the Act and of all the powers enabling it in this behalf.
Yours faithfully,
(Dimple Bhandia) Chief General Manager

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
FINANCIAL MARKETS REGULATION DEPARTMENT Notification No. FMRD.DIRD.10/14.02.001/2023-24 dated January 03, 2024
Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Paper and Non- Convertible Debentures of original or initial maturity upto one year) Directions,
In exercise of the powers conferred under section 45J, 45K, 45L and section 45W of
the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 (hereinafter called the Act) read with section 45U
of the Act and in supersession of Notification No. FMD.MSRG.49/02.13.016/2010-
2011 dated July 28, 2010, Section IV of FMRD. Master Direction No. 2/2016-17 dated
July 07, 2016 and Direction No. FMRD.DIRD.01/CGM (TRS) - 2017 dated August 10,
2017, the Reserve Bank of India (hereinafter called the Reserve Bank) hereby issues
the following Directions.

children.title: (a) These Directions shall be called the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India
children.text:
(a) These Directions shall be called the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India
(Commercial Paper and Non-Convertible Debentures of original or initial
maturity upto one year) Directions, 2024.

children.title: (b) These Directions shall be applicable to all persons/agencies dealing in
children.text:
(b) These Directions shall be applicable to all persons/agencies dealing in
Commercial Paper and/or Non-Convertible Debentures of original or initial
maturity upto one year.

children.title: (c) These Directions shall come into force with effect from April 01, 2024.
children.text: (c) These Directions shall come into force with effect from April 01, 2024.

children.title: (a) For the purpose of these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires:
children.text: (a) For the purpose of these Directions, unless the context otherwise requires:

children.title: (i) All India Financial Institution (AIFI) shall include: (a) Export Import Bank
children.text:
(i) All India Financial Institution (AIFI) shall include: (a) Export Import Bank
of India, (b) National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development, (c)
National Housing Bank, (d) Small Industries Development Bank of India
and (e) National Bank for Financing Infrastructure and Development.

children.title: (ii) Bank means a banking company (including a Payment Bank and a Small
children.text:
(ii) Bank means a banking company (including a Payment Bank and a Small
Finance Bank) as defined in clause (c) of Section 5 of the Banking
Regulation Act, 1949 (10 of 1949) or a "regional rural bank", a
"corresponding new bank" or "State Bank of India" as defined in clauses
(ja), (da) and (nc), of section 5 respectively thereof, or a "cooperative bank"
as defined in clause (cci) of Section 5 read with Section 56 of the said Act.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (iii) Body corporate means any entity incorporated by or under any Statute
children.text:
(iii) Body corporate means any entity incorporated by or under any Statute
for the time being in force but does not include a co-operative society
registered under any law relating to co-operative societies.

children.title: (iv) Commercial Paper (CP) means an unsecured money market
children.text:
(iv) Commercial Paper (CP) means an unsecured money market
instrument issued in the form of a promissory note.

children.title: (v) Company means a company as defined in Section 2 (20) of the
children.text:
(v) Company means a company as defined in Section 2 (20) of the
Companies Act, 2013 (18 of 2013).

children.title: (vi) Co-operative society shall have the meaning as assigned to it under
children.text:
(vi) Co-operative society shall have the meaning as assigned to it under
clause (cciia) of Section 5 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 read with
Section 56 of the said Act.

children.title: (vii) Delivery versus Payment (DvP) means a settlement mechanism which
children.text:
(vii) Delivery versus Payment (DvP) means a settlement mechanism which
stipulates that transfer of funds from the buyer of securities is made
simultaneously with the transfer of securities by the seller of securities.

children.title: (viii) Depository shall have the meaning as assigned in Section 2 (e) of the
children.text:
(viii) Depository shall have the meaning as assigned in Section 2 (e) of the
Depositories Act, 1996 (22 of 1996).

children.title: (ix) Debenture Trustee means an entity registered with SEBI as debenture
children.text:
(ix) Debenture Trustee means an entity registered with SEBI as debenture
trustee under the SEBI (Debenture Trustees) Regulations, 1993.

children.title: (x) Electronic Trading Platform (ETP) shall have the meaning as
children.text:
(x) Electronic Trading Platform (ETP) shall have the meaning as
assigned in paragraph 2(1) (iii) of the Electronic Trading Platforms
(Reserve Bank) Directions, 2018 dated October 05, 2018 or as modified
from time to time.

children.title: (xi) Financial Benchmark Administrator (FBA) means a person who
children.text:
(xi) Financial Benchmark Administrator (FBA) means a person who
controls the creation, operation and administration of financial
benchmark(s) authorized under the Reserve Bank of India (Financial
Benchmark Administrators) Directions, 2023, dated December 28, 2023,
as amended from time to time.

children.title: (xii) Group entities means an arrangement involving two or more entities
children.text:
(xii) Group entities means an arrangement involving two or more entities
related to each other through any of the following relationships: (a)
subsidiary - parent (defined in terms of Ind-AS 110/AS 21); (b) joint
venture (defined in terms of Ind-AS 28/AS 27) ; (c) associate (defined in
terms of Ind-AS 28/AS 23); (d) Promoter-promotee (as provided in the
SEBI (Acquisition of Shares and Takeover) Regulations, 1997) for listed
companies; (e) common brand name or (f) investment in equity shares
of 20 per cent and above.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (xiii) Infrastructure Investment Trust (InvIT) means a business trust as
children.text:
(xiii) Infrastructure Investment Trust (InvIT) means a business trust as
defined in sub-clause (i) of clause 13A of section 2 of the Income-tax Act,
1961.

children.title: (xiv) Issuing and Paying Agent (IPA) means a Scheduled Commercial Bank
children.text:
(xiv) Issuing and Paying Agent (IPA) means a Scheduled Commercial Bank
undertaking duties and responsibilities specified under paragraph 7(a)
of these Directions.

children.title: (xv) Limited liability partnership shall have the meaning as assigned in
children.text:
(xv) Limited liability partnership shall have the meaning as assigned in
Section 2(n) of the Limited Liability Partnership Act, 2008 (6 of 2009).

children.title: (xvi) Money market instruments shall have the meaning as assigned in
children.text:
(xvi) Money market instruments shall have the meaning as assigned in
Section 45(U) (b) of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

children.title: (xvii) Non-Banking Financial Company (NBFC) means a company as
children.text:
(xvii) Non-Banking Financial Company (NBFC) means a company as
defined in Section 45 I (f) of the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

children.title: (xviii) Non-Convertible Debenture (NCD) means a secured money market
children.text:
(xviii) Non-Convertible Debenture (NCD) means a secured money market
instrument with an original or initial maturity upto one year.

children.title: (xix) A Non-resident shall mean a 'person resident outside India' and shall
children.text:
(xix) A Non-resident shall mean a 'person resident outside India' and shall
have the meaning as assigned to it in section 2 (w) of Foreign Exchange
Management Act, 1999 (42 of 1999).

children.title: (xx) Over-the-Counter (OTC) markets refers to markets where transactions
children.text:
(xx) Over-the-Counter (OTC) markets refers to markets where transactions
are undertaken in any manner other than on recognised stock
exchanges and shall include those undertaken on electronic trading
platforms (ETPs).

children.title: (xxi) A Promoter shall have the meaning as assigned in Section 2(69) of the
children.text:
(xxi) A Promoter shall have the meaning as assigned in Section 2(69) of the
Companies Act, 2013.

children.title: (xxii) Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) means a business trust as
children.text:
(xxii) Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) means a business trust as
defined in sub-clause (ii) of clause 13A of section 2 of the Income-tax Act,
1961.

children.title: (xxiii) Recognised stock exchanges shall have the meaning as assigned in
children.text:
(xxiii) Recognised stock exchanges shall have the meaning as assigned in
Section 2 (f) of the Securities Contracts Regulation Act, 1956 (42 of
1956).

children.title: (xxiv) Related parties shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under
children.text:
(xxiv) Related parties shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under
Indian Accounting Standard (Ind AS) 24 - Related Party Disclosures or
International Accounting Standard (IAS) 24 - Related Party Disclosures
or any other equivalent accounting standards.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (xxv) Resident shall mean a 'Person resident in India' and shall have the
children.text:
(xxv) Resident shall mean a 'Person resident in India' and shall have the
same meaning as assigned to it in Section 2 (v) of the Foreign Exchange
Management Act, 1999 (42 of 1999).

children.title: (b) Words and expressions used but not defined in these Directions shall have the
children.text:
(b) Words and expressions used but not defined in these Directions shall have the
meaning assigned to them in the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

children.title: (a) CPs and NCDs may be issued by the following entities subject to the condition
children.text:
(a) CPs and NCDs may be issued by the following entities subject to the condition
that all fund-based facilities availed, if any, by the issuer from banks/ AIFIs /
NBFCs are classified as Standard at the time of issue:

children.title: (i) Companies;
children.text: (i) Companies;

children.title: (ii) NBFCs, including Housing Finance Companies (HFCs);
children.text: (ii) NBFCs, including Housing Finance Companies (HFCs);

children.title: (iii) InvITs and REITs;
children.text: (iii) InvITs and REITs;

children.title: (iv) All India Financial Institutions (AIFIs);
children.text: (iv) All India Financial Institutions (AIFIs);

children.title: (v) Any other body corporate with a minimum net-worth of ₹100 crore,
children.text:
(v) Any other body corporate with a minimum net-worth of ₹100 crore,
provided that the body corporate is statutorily permitted to incur debt or
issue debt instruments in India; and

children.title: (vi) Any other entity specifically permitted by the Reserve Bank.
children.text: (vi) Any other entity specifically permitted by the Reserve Bank.

children.title: (b) Co-operative societies and limited liability partnerships with a minimum net-
children.text:
(b) Co-operative societies and limited liability partnerships with a minimum net-
worth of ₹100 crore, may also issue CPs under these Directions, subject to the
condition that all fund-based facilities availed, if any, by the issuer from banks/
AIFIs / NBFCs are classified as Standard at the time of issue.

children.title: (a) All residents are eligible to invest in CPs and NCDs.
children.text: (a) All residents are eligible to invest in CPs and NCDs.

children.title: (b) Non-residents are eligible to invest in CPs and NCDs to the extent permitted
children.text:
(b) Non-residents are eligible to invest in CPs and NCDs to the extent permitted
under Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA), 1999 or the
rules/regulations framed thereunder.
Provided that no person, resident or non-resident, can invest in CPs and NCDs
issued by related parties either in the primary or through the secondary market.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (a) Primary Issuance
children.text: (a) Primary Issuance

children.title: (i) CPs and NCDs shall be issued in dematerialised form and held with a
children.text:
(i) CPs and NCDs shall be issued in dematerialised form and held with a
depository registered with SEBI.

children.title: (ii) CPs and NCDs shall be issued in minimum denomination of ₹5 lakh and
children.text:
(ii) CPs and NCDs shall be issued in minimum denomination of ₹5 lakh and
in multiples of ₹5 lakh thereafter.

children.title: (iii) The tenor of a CP shall not be less than seven days or more than one
children.text:
(iii) The tenor of a CP shall not be less than seven days or more than one
year. The tenor of an NCD shall not be less than ninety days or more
than one year.

children.title: (iv) Issuance of a CP/NCD with options (call/put) is not permitted.
children.text: (iv) Issuance of a CP/NCD with options (call/put) is not permitted.

children.title: (v) Issuance of a CP/NCD is not permitted to be underwritten or co-
children.text:
(v) Issuance of a CP/NCD is not permitted to be underwritten or co-
accepted.

children.title: (vi) The offer documents for the issue of CPs and NCDs shall, at the
children.text:
(vi) The offer documents for the issue of CPs and NCDs shall, at the
minimum, include disclosures as given in Annex I.

children.title: (vii) The primary issuances of CPs and NCDs, including both payment of
children.text:
(vii) The primary issuances of CPs and NCDs, including both payment of
funds to the issuer and issue of CPs and NCDs to the investors, shall be
settled within a period not exceeding T+4 working days, where T
represents the deal date, i.e., the date on which the trade details,
including price/rate are agreed by the issuer and the investor(s).

children.title: (viii) Total subscription by all individuals, including Hindu Undivided Families,
children.text:
(viii) Total subscription by all individuals, including Hindu Undivided Families,
in any primary issuance of CPs or NCDs shall not exceed 25 per cent of
the total amount issued.

children.title: (b) Discount/Coupon Rate
children.text: (b) Discount/Coupon Rate

children.title: (i) CPs shall be issued at a discount to the face value.
children.text: (i) CPs shall be issued at a discount to the face value.

children.title: (ii) NCDs shall be issued at a discount to the face value or with fixed or
children.text:
(ii) NCDs shall be issued at a discount to the face value or with fixed or
floating rate coupon.

children.title: (iii) The coupon on floating rate NCDs shall be linked to a benchmark
children.text:
(iii) The coupon on floating rate NCDs shall be linked to a benchmark
published by a Financial Benchmark Administrator or approved by the
Fixed Income Money Market and Derivatives Association of India
(FIMMDA) for this purpose. FIMMDA shall ensure that any floating rate
approved by them for this purpose is determined transparently,
objectively and in arms' length transactions. The coupon on floating rate
NCDs can also be linked to policy rates published by the Reserve Bank.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (c) Credit Enhancement
children.text: (c) Credit Enhancement

children.title: (i) Banks and AIFIs may, based on their commercial judgement and subject
children.text:
(i) Banks and AIFIs may, based on their commercial judgement and subject
to prudential guidelines issued by Department of Regulation, RBI,
choose to provide stand-by assistance/credit, back-stop facility, etc., by
way of credit enhancement for a CP/NCD issue.

children.title: (ii) Non-bank entities (including corporates) may provide unconditional and
children.text:
(ii) Non-bank entities (including corporates) may provide unconditional and
irrevocable guarantee for credit enhancement of CPs and NCDs issued
by a group entity subject to making appropriate disclosures as set out in

children.title: (d) End-use
children.text: (d) End-use

children.title: (i) Funds raised through CPs and NCDs shall ordinarily be used to finance
children.text:
(i) Funds raised through CPs and NCDs shall ordinarily be used to finance
current assets and operating expenses. The end-use of the funds raised
through a CP or an NCD shall be disclosed in the offer document.

children.title: (ii) Where funds raised are used for purposes other than financing current
children.text:
(ii) Where funds raised are used for purposes other than financing current
assets and operating expenses, the exact/ specific end-use shall be
disclosed in the offer document.

children.title: (iii) The issuer shall submit a certificate from the Chief Executive
children.text:
(iii) The issuer shall submit a certificate from the Chief Executive
Officer/Chief Financial Officer (CEO/CFO) to the IPAs concerned that
the proceeds of CPs and NCDs have been used for the disclosed
purposes and that all other provisions of these Directions and conditions
of the offer document have been adhered to. The certificate shall be
provided to the IPA within 3 months of the issue of CP/NCD or on
maturity of the issue, whichever is earlier.

children.title: (e) Rating Requirement
children.text:
(e) Rating Requirement
The minimum credit rating, assigned by a Credit Rating Agency (CRA), for the
issuance of CPs and NCDs shall be 'A3' as per rating symbol and definition
prescribed by SEBI.

children.title: (f) Primary Market - Other Conditions
children.text: (f) Primary Market - Other Conditions

children.title: (i) An IPA shall be appointed for each issuance of a CP and an NCD. A
children.text:
(i) An IPA shall be appointed for each issuance of a CP and an NCD. A
Debenture Trustee shall also be appointed for each issuance of an NCD.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ii) The subscription to the primary issue of a CP/NCD shall be routed
children.text:
(ii) The subscription to the primary issue of a CP/NCD shall be routed
through the IPA.

children.title: (iii) The aggregate amount of CPs and NCDs which can be issued by an
children.text:
(iii) The aggregate amount of CPs and NCDs which can be issued by an
issuer shall be within such limits as may be approved by the Board of
Directors or its equivalent body. The aggregate amount which can be
issued by an issuer regulated by a financial sector regulator shall also be
subject to the limits, if any, specified by the regulator concerned.

children.title: (g) Secondary Market-Trading venue and settlement
children.text: (g) Secondary Market-Trading venue and settlement

children.title: (i) CPs and NCDs shall be traded either in OTC markets, including on ETPs,
children.text:
(i) CPs and NCDs shall be traded either in OTC markets, including on ETPs,
or on recognised stock exchanges, approved by the Reserve Bank for the
purpose.

children.title: (ii) The settlement cycle for OTC trades in CPs and NCDs shall be either
children.text:
(ii) The settlement cycle for OTC trades in CPs and NCDs shall be either
T+0 or T+1.

children.title: (iii) All OTC secondary market transactions in CPs (including transactions
children.text:
(iii) All OTC secondary market transactions in CPs (including transactions
undertaken on ETPs) shall be settled on a DvP basis through the clearing
corporation of any recognised stock exchange, or any other mechanism
approved by the Reserve Bank.

children.title: (iv) All OTC secondary market transactions in NCDs (including transactions
children.text:
(iv) All OTC secondary market transactions in NCDs (including transactions
undertaken on ETPs) shall be settled bilaterally, or on a DvP basis
through the clearing corporation of any recognised stock exchange, or
any other mechanism approved by the Reserve Bank.

children.title: (h) Buyback
children.text:
(h) Buyback
Issuers of CPs and NCDs are permitted to buyback the CPs and NCDs before
maturity. Such buybacks shall be subject to the following conditions:

children.title: (i) The buyback of CPs can be made only after seven days from the date of
children.text:
(i) The buyback of CPs can be made only after seven days from the date of
issue. The buyback of NCDs can be made only after ninety days from the
date of issue.

children.title: (ii) The buyback offer shall be extended to all investors in a particular issue
children.text:
(ii) The buyback offer shall be extended to all investors in a particular issue
on identical terms and conditions. The investors shall have the option to
accept or reject the buyback offer.

children.title: (iii) Buyback of CPs and NCDs shall be at the prevailing market price.
children.text: (iii) Buyback of CPs and NCDs shall be at the prevailing market price.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (iv) The issuer of a CP/NCD shall inform the details of the buyback to the IPA
children.text:
(iv) The issuer of a CP/NCD shall inform the details of the buyback to the IPA
on the date of buyback. In the case of NCDs, the details shall also be
informed to the Debenture Trustee.

children.title: (v) The payment for the buyback of the CP/NCD by the issuer shall be routed
children.text:
(v) The payment for the buyback of the CP/NCD by the issuer shall be routed
through the IPA.

children.title: (vi) CPs and NCDs bought back, partially or in full, shall be extinguished on
children.text:
(vi) CPs and NCDs bought back, partially or in full, shall be extinguished on
the date of buyback.

children.title: (i) Repayment of CPs / NCDs
children.text: (i) Repayment of CPs / NCDs

children.title: (i) There will be no grace period for repayment of CPs/NCDs.
children.text: (i) There will be no grace period for repayment of CPs/NCDs.

children.title: (ii) The issuer shall make the funds for redemption available to the IPA by
children.text:
(ii) The issuer shall make the funds for redemption available to the IPA by
3:00 P.M. on the redemption date.

children.title: (iii) The repayment of a CP/NCD, including coupon payments, shall be routed
children.text:
(iii) The repayment of a CP/NCD, including coupon payments, shall be routed
through the IPA.

children.title: (j) Default
children.text: (j) Default

children.title: (i) The issuer who has defaulted on the repayment of a coupon/redemption,
children.text:
(i) The issuer who has defaulted on the repayment of a coupon/redemption,
partially or in full, of a CP and/or NCD shall inform the details of any
default in payments related to a CP/NCD to the IPA before 5:00 pm on
the date of the default. In the case of NCDs, the details shall also be
informed to the Debenture Trustee.

children.title: (ii) Information about any default in payments related a CP/NCD shall be
children.text:
(ii) Information about any default in payments related a CP/NCD shall be
publicly disseminated (e.g., through its website) by the issuer. Default
details shall also be publicly disseminated on the F-TRAC Trade
Repository Platform of Clearing Corporation of India Ltd ('F-TRAC
platform') as specified under Para 6(d) of these Directions.

children.title: (iii) Repayments of obligations under a defaulted CP or NCD can be made
children.text:
(iii) Repayments of obligations under a defaulted CP or NCD can be made
directly to the investor/s by the issuer or can be routed through the IPA
or Debenture Trustee. Partial repayments, if any, of a CP/NCD shall be
distributed to investors of the CP/NCD in proportion to the investment
made in the CP /NCD.

children.title: (iv) Details of the repayment of the obligations related to defaulted CPs/NCDs
children.text:
(iv) Details of the repayment of the obligations related to defaulted CPs/NCDs
shall be informed to the IPA and the Debenture Trustee by the issuer on
the date of the repayment.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) In the event of a CP/NCD, being converted into another financial
children.text:
(v) In the event of a CP/NCD, being converted into another financial
instrument after default, as part of any bilateral / multilateral agreement
or restructuring scheme, the CP/NCD shall stand extinguished on the
date of its conversion.

children.title: (vi) In the event of default of a CP / NCD, the issuer shall not be allowed to
children.text:
(vi) In the event of default of a CP / NCD, the issuer shall not be allowed to
issue CPs or NCDs till full repayment of the defaulted obligation or six
months after the date of default, whichever is earlier.

children.title: (vii) Any event of conversion of a CP/NCD into another financial instrument
children.text:
(vii) Any event of conversion of a CP/NCD into another financial instrument
shall be reported by the issuer to the IPA and the Debenture Trustee.

children.title: (k) Market timing
children.text:
(k) Market timing
Primary issuance and secondary market trading hours shall be between 9:00 AM
and 5:00 PM on a working day or as specified by the Reserve Bank from time to
time.

children.title: (l) Market Practices and Documentation
children.text:
(l) Market Practices and Documentation
Participants / agencies in the CP and NCD markets shall follow any standardised
procedures and documentation which may be prescribed by FIMMDA, in
consultation with the Reserve Bank, for smooth functioning of the markets.

children.title: (a) Primary issuances: Details of all issuances in primary markets of the CPs
children.text:
(a) Primary issuances: Details of all issuances in primary markets of the CPs
and NCDs shall be reported by the IPA on the F-TRAC platform by 5:30 PM
on the day of issuance.

children.title: (b) Secondary market transactions: All secondary market transactions in CPs
children.text:
(b) Secondary market transactions: All secondary market transactions in CPs
and NCDs, executed in the OTC market and/or on the recognised stock
exchanges, shall be reported with time stamp within 15 minutes of execution
(the time when price is agreed) on the F-TRAC platform by each counterparty
to the transaction.

children.title: (c) Buybacks: Details of buybacks of CPs and NCDs shall be reported by the
children.text:
(c) Buybacks: Details of buybacks of CPs and NCDs shall be reported by the
IPA on the F-TRAC platform by 5:30 PM on the buyback date.

children.title: (d) Default: Instances of default and repayment of defaulted obligation shall be
children.text:
(d) Default: Instances of default and repayment of defaulted obligation shall be
reported by the IPA on the F-TRAC platform by 5:30 PM on the day of default
or the day of repayment of defaulted obligations, as the case may be.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (e) Reporting by depositories: The depositories shall report to the Reserve
children.text:
(e) Reporting by depositories: The depositories shall report to the Reserve
Bank, the details of the CPs and NCDs held with them in the dematerialised
form, in the prescribed format furnished in Annex II, at fortnightly intervals (on
the 15th day and on the last day of the month) or as and when called upon to
do so by the Reserve Bank.

children.title: (f) Reporting by Debenture Trustee: The Debenture Trustee shall report the
children.text:
(f) Reporting by Debenture Trustee: The Debenture Trustee shall report the
details of the outstanding amount of NCDs and the particulars of default in
repayment of NCD, at quarterly intervals (within 15 days from the end of the
quarter), in the format prescribed in Annex III to the Reserve Bank through e-
mail (reportfmd@rbi.org.in).

hierarchy.title: 7. Roles and responsibilities
hierarchy.text:
7. Roles and responsibilities
The roles and responsibilities of the IPA, Debenture Trustee and Credit Rating
Agency (CRA) with respect to the operations in CP and NCD markets are set
out below:

children.title: (a) Issuing and Paying Agent: The IPA for an issuance shall:
children.text: (a) Issuing and Paying Agent: The IPA for an issuance shall:

children.title: (i) Ensure that the issuer of a CP/NCD is authorised to borrow through CPs
children.text:
(i) Ensure that the issuer of a CP/NCD is authorised to borrow through CPs
and/or NCDs and that the issuance is in compliance with these Directions.

children.title: (ii) Verify and hold certified copies of original documents and/or digitally
children.text:
(ii) Verify and hold certified copies of original documents and/or digitally
signed documents related to the issuance in its custody.

children.title: (iii) Issue an IPA certificate that all information and documents submitted by
children.text:
(iii) Issue an IPA certificate that all information and documents submitted by
the issuer are in order.

children.title: (iv) Make available the IPA certificate in electronic form on the website of the
children.text:
(iv) Make available the IPA certificate in electronic form on the website of the
depositories for the CPs or NCDs issued.

children.title: (v) Obtain the certificate referred to in Para 5(d)(iii) of these Directions from
children.text:
(v) Obtain the certificate referred to in Para 5(d)(iii) of these Directions from
the CEO/CFO of the issuer.

children.title: (vi) Ensure that the reporting obligations specified in these Directions are
children.text:
(vi) Ensure that the reporting obligations specified in these Directions are
complied with.

children.title: (vii) The Reserve Bank may, in the event of an IPA violating any provision of
children.text:
(vii) The Reserve Bank may, in the event of an IPA violating any provision of
these directions, or any other directions/ regulations/guidelines issued
by the Bank from time to time in this regard, disallow an entity from acting
as IPA for CP/NCD issuances for a period, as may be decided by the
Reserve Bank.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (b) Debenture Trustee
children.text: (b) Debenture Trustee

children.title: (i) The roles, responsibilities, duties and functions of the Debenture
children.text:
(i) The roles, responsibilities, duties and functions of the Debenture
Trustees shall be guided by the Securities and Exchange Board of India
(Debenture Trustees) Regulations, 1993, the provisions of Companies
Act, 2013 related to Debenture Trustees, as applicable, the trust deed
and the offer document.

children.title: (ii) The Debenture Trustee shall ensure that the reporting obligations
children.text:
(ii) The Debenture Trustee shall ensure that the reporting obligations
specified in these Directions are complied with.

children.title: (iii) The Debenture Trustee shall submit to the Reserve Bank any
children.text:
(iii) The Debenture Trustee shall submit to the Reserve Bank any
information regarding NCDs, as may be required by the Reserve Bank
from time to time.

children.title: (iv) The Reserve Bank may, in the event of a Debenture Trustee violating
children.text:
(iv) The Reserve Bank may, in the event of a Debenture Trustee violating
any provision of these directions, or any other directions/
regulations/guidelines issued by the Bank from time to time in this
regard, disallow an entity to act as Debenture Trustee for an NCD
issuance for a period, as may be decided by the Reserve Bank.

children.title: (c) Credit Rating Agency
children.text: (c) Credit Rating Agency

children.title: (i) A CRA registered with SEBI and accredited by the Reserve Bank as
children.text:
(i) A CRA registered with SEBI and accredited by the Reserve Bank as
External Credit Assessment Institution (ECAI) for assigning bank loan
ratings shall be eligible to rate CPs and NCDs.

children.title: (ii) CRAs shall abide by the guidelines issued by SEBI as applicable to
children.text:
(ii) CRAs shall abide by the guidelines issued by SEBI as applicable to
securities mutatis mutandis for ratings of CPs/NCDs. They shall also
abide by any guidelines issued from time to time by the Reserve Bank in
this regard.

children.title: (iii) CRAs may also abide by any directions/regulations/guidelines issued by
children.text:
(iii) CRAs may also abide by any directions/regulations/guidelines issued by
any regulator or other authority in respect of rating of CPs and NCDs
provided that such directions/regulations/guidelines do not conflict with
these Directions or any guidelines issued from time to time by the
Reserve Bank in this regard.

children.title: (iv) A CRA which has been deregistered by SEBI or dis-accredited by the
children.text:
(iv) A CRA which has been deregistered by SEBI or dis-accredited by the
Reserve Bank as an ECAI for assigning bank loans ratings, shall no
longer be eligible to rate CPs/ NCDs. The Reserve Bank may also, in the

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
event of a CRA violating any provision of these directions, or any other
guidelines issued by the Bank from time to time in this regard, disallow
the CRA from rating CPs / NCDs for a period, as may be decided by the
Reserve Bank.

hierarchy.title: 8. Obligation to provide information sought by the Reserve Bank: The Reserve
hierarchy.text:
8. Obligation to provide information sought by the Reserve Bank: The Reserve
Bank may call for any information or seek any clarification from any agency involved
in the CP and NCD markets, including but not limited to, issuers, investors, IPAs,
debenture trustees, CRAs, depositories, the clearing corporations and the stock
exchanges, which in the opinion of the Reserve Bank is relevant and the agency shall
furnish such additional information and clarification within the time frame specified.

hierarchy.title: 9. Dissemination of data: The Reserve Bank or any other agency authorised by it,
hierarchy.text:
9. Dissemination of data: The Reserve Bank or any other agency authorised by it,
may, in public interest, publish any anonymised data related to primary and secondary
market transactions in CPs and NCDs.

hierarchy.title: 10. Violation of Directions: In the event of any person violating any provision of these
hierarchy.text:
10. Violation of Directions: In the event of any person violating any provision of these
Directions, the Reserve Bank may, in addition to taking any penal or regulatory action
in accordance with law, disallow that person from participating in the CP and NCD
markets for a period not exceeding one month at a time, after providing reasonable
opportunity to the entity to defend its actions, and such action would be made public
by the Reserve Bank.

hierarchy.title: 11. Applicability of other laws, directions, regulations or guidelines: Participants
hierarchy.text:
11. Applicability of other laws, directions, regulations or guidelines: Participants
in the CP and NCD markets shall abide by the provisions of any direction, regulation,
or guideline issued by any other regulator or authority, that may be applicable, in
respect of issue of or investment in CPs and NCDs, provided that such directions,
regulations or guidelines do not conflict with these Directions. In case of any conflicts,
the provisions of these Directions shall prevail.

hierarchy.title: 12. Non-applicability of Certain Other Directions: Nothing contained in the Master
hierarchy.text:
12. Non-applicability of Certain Other Directions: Nothing contained in the Master
Direction - Non-Banking Financial Companies Acceptance of Public Deposits
(Reserve Bank) Directions, 2016, as updated from time to time, shall apply to the
raising of funds by issuance of CPs, by any NBFC when such funds are raised in
accordance with these Directions.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
13.
These Directions shall apply to transactions in CPs and NCDs entered into from
the date the Directions come into effect. Provisions of Section IV of FMRD.Master
Direction No.
2/2016-17 dated July 07, 2016, Notification No.
FMD.MSRG.49/02.13.016/2010-2011 dated July 28, 2010 and Direction No.
FMRD.DIRD.01/CGM (TRS) - 2017 dated August 10, 2017 will continue to be
applicable to the CPs and NCDs issued in accordance with the said Directions till the
maturity of those CPs and NCDs.
(Dimple Bhandia)
Chief General Manager

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

hierarchy.title: Annex I
hierarchy.text:
Annex I
Minimum disclosure in the offer document by issuers of CP and NCD
i. Details of outstanding CPs, NCDs and other debt instruments as on date of
offer letter, including amount issued, maturity date, amount outstanding, credit
rating and name of credit rating agency for the issue, name of IPA and
Debenture Trustee.
ii. Net-worth of the issuer as per the latest balance sheet.
iii. Shareholding of the issuer's promoters and the details of the shares pledged
by the promoters, if any.
iv. Long term credit rating, if any, obtained by the issuer.
v. Unaccepted credit ratings, if any, assigned to the issuer.
vi. Summary of audited financials of last three years, material litigation and
regulatory actions related to the issuer. If the issuer has not been in existence
for three years, the information of the issuer for the period such information is
available shall be disclosed.
vii. Any material event/ development having implications for the financials/ credit
quality resulting in material liabilities, corporate restructuring event which may
affect the issue or the investor's decision to invest in the CP/NCD.
viii. All details of credit enhancement including backstop facilities provided by the
group entity including but not limited to (a) the net-worth of the guarantor, (b)
the names of the companies to which the guarantor has issued similar
guarantees, (c) the extent of the guarantees offered by the guarantor and (d)
the conditions under which the guarantee will be invoked, etc.
ix. Details of default of CP, NCD or any other debt instrument and other financial
indebtedness including corporate guarantee issued in the past five financial
years including in the current financial year.
x. Details of statutory auditor and changes thereof in the last three financial
years.
xi. Details of current tranche including amount, current credit rating for the issue,
name of credit rating agency, its validity period and details of IPA and
Debenture Trustee.
xii. Specific details of end-use of funds.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
xiii. An issuer which is either an NBFC or an HFC shall disclose the residual
maturity profile of its assets and liabilities in the following format:
